title: China Targets Growth Again
summary:
Main
By Ulf Bergman
Many commodity traders probably breathed a sigh of relief when the Chinese Premier Li Keqiang said that the country would strive for at least six per cent of GDP growth in 2021 during a recent speech delivering the government's annual work report. While the target may be a bit on the modest side by Chinese standards, some pundits were expecting the country to refrain from a specific growth target for the second year in a row and the statement put such fears to rest. A failure to set a growth target for the year would have been a bearish sign for the commodity markets, signalling a lack of faith in the strength of the economy by the Chinese leadership.
